name: sim_carnot_axis0_entropy_gradient_bridge
claim: Carnot cycle delta-S at each step co-varies with Axis 0 (entropy gradient / distinguishability cost). Isothermal steps = signed Axis 0 moves; adiabatic steps = Axis 0 flat; full reversible cycle = closed loop on Axis 0 manifold.

pytorch.reason: autograd computes dS/dT (entropy gradient w.r.t. temperature); load-bearing for Axis-0 gradient test

pyg.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

z3.reason: UNSAT proof that T_H < T_C AND eta > 0 is impossible (Second Law as z3 constraint); load-bearing

cvc5.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

sympy.reason: symbolic derivation of eta = 1 - T_C/T_H; d(eta)/d(T_C) = -1/T_H verified; load-bearing

clifford.reason: Carnot cycle as closed path in Cl(2,0): +e1 (entropy up), -e1 (entropy down), e2 (adiabatic); closure verified; load-bearing

geomstats.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

e3nn.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

rustworkx.reason: directed graph of 4 Carnot steps; Axis-0-active nodes (step1, step3) identified by degree analysis; load-bearing

xgi.reason: hyperedge {step1, step3, axis0} — entropy-active steps form a hyperedge with Axis 0; load-bearing

toponetx.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

gudhi.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

P1_isothermal_expansion_dS_positive.note: Carnot step 1: system absorbs heat Q_H at T_H; dS = Q_H/T_H > 0 => Axis 0 uphill

P2_isothermal_compression_dS_negative.note: Carnot step 3: system expels heat Q_C at T_C; dS = -Q_C/T_C < 0 => Axis 0 downhill

P3_adiabatic_steps_dS_zero.note: Carnot steps 2,4: adiabatic, no heat exchange => dS = 0 => Axis 0 flat (constraint surface traversal)

P4_reversible_cycle_closure.note: Reversible Carnot: total entropy change = 0 => closed loop on Axis 0 manifold

P5_pytorch_autograd_entropy_gradient.note: autograd confirms dS/dT != 0 for both isothermal steps => Axis 0 gradient is real

P6_sympy_efficiency_gradient.note: d(eta)/d(T_C) = -1/T_H => lower cold reservoir = steeper Axis 0 gradient

P7_rustworkx_axis0_active_nodes.note: Steps 1 and 3 are Axis-0-active (entropy changes); steps 2 and 4 are inactive

P8_xgi_axis0_hyperedge.note: Entropy-active steps {step1, step3} form a hyperedge with Axis 0

P9_clifford_cycle_closure.note: Carnot cycle as Cl(2,0) path: +e1 + e2 + (-e1) + (-e2) = 0 => closed loop

N1_z3_second_law_unsat.note: UNSAT: T_H < T_C AND eta > 0 is impossible — Second Law as z3 constraint

N2_irreversible_cycle_dS_positive.note: Irreversible cycle: dS_universe = -Q_H/T_H + Q_C/T_C > 0 => open trajectory on Axis 0 manifold

N3_intermediate_temp_degraded_efficiency.note: Using intermediate T_work < T_C -> T_H degrades efficiency: excluded from Axis 0 maximum

N4_spurious_heat_in_adiabatic_breaks_fla.note: Adiabatic step with Q != 0 produces dS != 0 => Axis 0 is not flat (breaks constraint)

N5_sympy_eta_increases_with_TH.note: eta increases with T_H (d_eta/dT_H > 0): higher hot reservoir = steeper positive Axis 0 gradient

B1_Tc_to_zero_eta_to_one.note: T_C -> 0: eta -> 1 (Axis 0 maximally biased); never exactly 1 at finite T_C

B2_TH_eq_TC_eta_zero.note: T_H = T_C: eta = 0 => Axis 0 gradient is zero (no distinguishability cost difference)

B3_pytorch_gradient_at_equal_temps.note: At T_C = T_H: d(eta)/d(T_H) = T_C/T_H^2 still nonzero (gradient exists, but efficiency = 0)

B4_clifford_isothermal_steps_antiparalle.note: Isothermal steps 1 and 3 are anti-parallel in Cl(2,0): one is Axis 0 uphill, other is downhill

B5_xgi_adiabatic_steps_exclude_axis0_fro.note: Adiabatic pair forms size-2 hyperedge (no Axis 0); isothermal pair forms size-3 with Axis 0
